---
title: "sorghum_variant_calling"
author: "subhash"
date: "2025-10-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir=normalizePath('..//..//'))
```

# 1. Convert CRAM to FASTQ

Driver script to run CRAM to FASTQ conversion:

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
set -euo pipefail

WORK=/mnt/nrdstor/jyanglab/subhash/sorghum_WGS_JS
SCRIPT=$WORK/sorghum_cram_to_fastq.sh
FASTQ_ROOT=/work/jyanglab/subhash/1.fastq
LOG_DIR=/work/jyanglab/subhash/logs
LIST=$FASTQ_ROOT/piscbt_crams.list

mkdir -p "$FASTQ_ROOT" "$LOG_DIR"

find "$WORK" -maxdepth 3 -type f -name '*.cram' \
    | grep -E '/(PI|SC|unknown|BT)[^/]*/[^/]+\.cram$' \
    | grep -v '_unmatched\.cram$' \
    | sort > "$LIST"

while read -r CRAM; do
    SAMPLE=$(basename "${CRAM%.cram}")
    sbatch --export=CRAM="$CRAM",SAMPLE="$SAMPLE" "$SCRIPT"
done < "$LIST"
```

Conversion script:

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=8
#SBATCH --mem=16G
#SBATCH --time=24:00:00
#SBATCH --partition=jclarke,batch,guest
#SBATCH --job-name=cram2fq
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=smahamkalivenkatas2@huskers.unl.edu
#SBATCH --output=/work/jyanglab/subhash/logs/%x_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=/work/jyanglab/subhash/logs/%x_%j.err

module load samtools pigz 2>/dev/null || module load samtools

REF=/work/jyanglab/subhash/refs/hg38
WORK=/mnt/nrdstor/jyanglab/subhash/sorghum_WGS_JS
FASTQ_ROOT=/work/jyanglab/subhash/1.fastq
LOG_DIR=/work/jyanglab/subhash/logs
CRAM=${CRAM:?Set CRAM path via sbatch --export}
SAMPLE=${SAMPLE:-$(basename "${CRAM%.cram}")}
FASTQ_DIR="$FASTQ_ROOT/$SAMPLE"

mkdir -p "$FASTQ_DIR" "$LOG_DIR"

samtools view -@ 8 -T "$REF/hg38.fa" -b "$CRAM" \
    | samtools fastq -@ 8 - \
        -1 "$FASTQ_DIR/${SAMPLE}_R1.fq" \
        -2 "$FASTQ_DIR/${SAMPLE}_R2.fq" \
        -0 "$FASTQ_DIR/${SAMPLE}_unpaired.fq" \
        -s "$FASTQ_DIR/${SAMPLE}_single.fq" \
        -n

for fq in \
    "$FASTQ_DIR/${SAMPLE}_R1.fq" \
    "$FASTQ_DIR/${SAMPLE}_R2.fq" \
    "$FASTQ_DIR/${SAMPLE}_unpaired.fq" \
    "$FASTQ_DIR/${SAMPLE}_single.fq"; do
  [[ -f "$fq" ]] || : > "$fq"
  pigz -p 8 "$fq" || gzip "$fq"
done
```

# 2. Trim Low-Quality Reads and Adapters with fastp

The repository preserves the exact fastp scripts under:

- `scripts/work/jyanglab/subhash/NEW/1.fastq/fastp_unpaired_array.sh`
- `scripts/work/jyanglab/subhash/NEW/1.fastq/prepare_fastp_unpaired_array.sh`
- `scripts/work/jyanglab/subhash/NEW/1.fastq/run_fastp_unpaired.sh`

The key sample list is:

- `metadata/work/jyanglab/subhash/NEW/1.fastq/trim/unpaired_fastq.list`

# 3. Align to Reference Genome with bwa-mem2

Main script:

- `scripts/work/jyanglab/subhash/NEW/scripts/alignment_unpaired_array.sh`

Important settings:

- Reference FASTA:
  `/work/jyanglab/subhash/variant_calling/0.test/1.Raw_reads/4.refrence/Phytozome/PhytozomeV13/Sbicolor/v5.1/assembly/Sorghum_bicolorv5.Sb-BTX623-REFERENCE-JGI-5.1.dna.toplevel.fa`
- Modules: `bwa-mem2`, `samtools`
- Mapping filter: `samtools view -q 30 -F 4`
- Output pattern: `/work/jyanglab/subhash/NEW/2.alignment/<sample>/<sample>.srt.bam`

# 4. Picard MarkDuplicates

Main script:

- `scripts/work/jyanglab/subhash/NEW/scripts/picard_markduplicates_array.sh`

Important settings:

- Module: `picard/3.0`
- Duplicate handling: `REMOVE_DUPLICATES=true`
- Output pattern:
  `/work/jyanglab/subhash/NEW/picard/<sample>/<sample>_picard_dedup.bam`
